caseStudy.title: Web Development Optimization
caseStudy.description: A brief overview of case study 1.
caseStudy.content: Full content of Case Study 1 goes here.
caseStudy.overview: This project focused on a comprehensive overhaul of frontend performance for a large-scale web application. The main objectives were to reduce JavaScript bundle sizes, optimize image delivery, and streamline the user interface for a smoother experience. By analyzing user interaction patterns and performance bottlenecks, the team identified key areas for improvement to ensure faster load times and a more responsive application.
caseStudy.problem: The application suffered from bloated JavaScript bundles exceeding 4MB, unoptimized images causing slow initial loads, and a legacy component architecture that hindered iterative improvements. Users on slower connections experienced load times of over 12 seconds, resulting in high bounce rates.
caseStudy.solution: Code splitting and lazy loading were introduced at the route level, reducing the initial bundle to under 400KB. Images were migrated to a CDN with automatic WebP conversion and responsive sizing. The component library was refactored to use tree-shakeable imports, and a performance budget was enforced in CI to prevent regressions.

results.summary: The redesigned frontend delivers a significantly faster experience across all devices. User engagement metrics improved substantially, and the performance budget guardrails ensure sustained quality over time.
